perturbation.original_text: 7.1 The SENDER undertakes the obligation to pay irrevocably and unconditionally the Service for the Contracted Capacity, twenty (20) calendar days at the latest, after ECOPETROL files in the offices of the SENDER the invoice for the provision of the Service.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1 The SENDER undertakes the obligation to pay irrevocably and unconditionally the Service for the Contracted Capacity, forty-five (45) calendar days at the latest, after ECOPETROL files in the offices of the SENDER the invoice for the provision of the Service.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment deadline from 20 to 45 calendar days. This contradicts Clause 7.9 and potentially 7.3, creating uncertainty regarding the payment terms and the calculation of late payment penalties. The payment deadline inconsistency directly affects the enforcement of payment obligations and could lead to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.9 The shipment tax shall be invoiced in Colombian pesos upon obtaining the corresponding liquidation from the Ministry of Mines and Energy and shall be paid to ECOPETROL by the SENDER, within the fifteen (15) calendar days after ECOPETROL files in the offices of the SENDER the corresponding bills or invoices.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline from 20 to 45 calendar days in Clause 7.1. This directly contradicts Clause 7.9, which specifies a payment deadline of 15 calendar days for the shipment tax. This inconsistency creates a clear conflict regarding the payment terms and renders the enforcement of payment obligations uncertain, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 7.8 The SENDER shall pay late interests on any unpaid amounts pursuant to the provisions set out by ECOPETROL in the Guidelines for Administration of Service Receivables ECP-UTE-G-008 or a document that modifies or supersedes it, which is an integral part of the Contract hereto as Annex 2.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.8 The SENDER shall pay late interests on any unpaid amounts pursuant to the provisions set out by ECOPETROL in the Guidelines for Administration of Service Receivables ECP-UTE-G-008 or a document that modifies or supersedes it, which is not an integral part of the Contract hereto as Annex 2.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the 'Guidelines for Administration of Service Receivables ECP-UTE-G-008' are *not* an integral part of the contract, directly conflicting with the original text that clearly states it is (Annex 2). This affects the clause's enforceability and creates ambiguity regarding which guidelines govern late interest payments, potentially leading to legal challenges and disputes as per Clause 12 where penalties for defaults are mentioned. In-text contradictions are being made on the terms and reference to those guidelines.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 In case of failing to fulfill the obligations of the SENDER as a result of any actions or illegal omissions or deviations from the Contract, the SENDER agrees to pay ECOPETROL as a penalty, an amount equivalent to ten percent (10%) of the final value of the Contract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and introduces a contradiction with clause 12.1. Originally, section 7.8 states that 'Guidelines for Administration of Service Receivables ECP-UTE-G-008' is an integral part of the contract as Annex 2. The changed text explicitly states that it is *not* an integral part of the contract. Clause 12.1 outlines penalties for failing to fulfill obligations as a result of deviations from the contract. By changing whether the guidelines are part of the contract, the document now contains conflicting information about which guidelines ECOPETROL can use to determine a penalty. This is a strong contradiction because it introduces ambiguity regarding which set of rules govern late interest payments, thus creating an in-text contradiction that affects enforceability as per Clause 12.1.

perturbation.original_text: 8.2 Otherwise, In order to guarantee compliance with all and each of the obligations of the SENDER under the Contract hereof, including but without being limited to the payment of the Fee, the SENDER is obliged to constitute in favor of ECOPETROL and to deliver within ten (10) business days after the subscription of the Contract hereof for the amount indicated in the Specific Conditions (hereinafter, the "Bond"): a) A performance policy for the payment of Services issued by an insurance policy legally established in the country, governed by the General Clauses of ECOPETROL indicated in Annex 3; or b) An irrevocable stand-by letter of credit at first requirement, issued by (i) a banking establishment authorized to operate in Colombia with AAA credit rating for its long-term debt in pesos, o (ii) a foreign financial entity with representation or a confirming and payment bank in Colombia, with risk credit of long term debt in dollars no less than the rating for the foreign sovereign debt of Colombia issued pursuant to the International Standby Practices (ISP98) of the International Chamber of Commerce, for which, it may be used the form contained in Annex 4 of the Contract hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.2 Otherwise, In order to guarantee compliance with all and each of the obligations of the SENDER under the Contract hereof, excluding the payment of the Fee, the SENDER is obliged to constitute in favor of ECOPETROL and to deliver within ten (10) business days after the subscription of the Contract hereof for the amount indicated in the Specific Conditions (hereinafter, the "Bond"): a) A performance policy for the payment of Services issued by an insurance policy legally established in the country, governed by the General Clauses of ECOPETROL indicated in Annex 3; or b) An irrevocable stand-by letter of credit at first requirement, issued by (i) a banking establishment authorized to operate in Colombia with AAA credit rating for its long-term debt in pesos, o (ii) a foreign financial entity with representation or a confirming and payment bank in Colombia, with risk credit of long term debt in dollars no less than the rating for the foreign sovereign debt of Colombia issued pursuant to the International Standby Practices (ISP98) of the International Chamber of Commerce, for which, it may be used the form contained in Annex 4 of the Contract hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction by explicitly *excluding* the 'payment of the Fee' from the obligations guaranteed by the bond, despite the original clause specifically including it. This contradicts clause 8.5, which links service provision to the validity of the bond, and introduces ambiguity on whether ECOPETROL can suspend service if the fee is not paid when the bond seemingly doesn't cover it. This in-text contradiction undermines the purpose of the bond and introduces legal risk.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.5 The issuance and validity of the Bond shall be an indispensable condition for the provision of the Service. As a consequence, ECOPETROL may suspend the provision of the Service or terminate the Contract in advance, when the Bond is not in force, without this waiving the SENDER from its payment obligations and all other obligations derived from the Contract hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 8.2 explicitly excludes the 'payment of the Fee' from the obligations guaranteed by the bond. Section 8.5 states "The issuance and validity of the Bond shall be an indispensable condition for the provision of the Service." If the bond does not guarantee the payment of the fee, as stated in the modified 8.2, this directly contradicts 8.5, creating uncertainty about Ecopetrol's ability to suspend service for non-payment of fees and therefore, a direct conflict.
